commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces OpenClaw image resize, encode, probe, and managed-attachment helpers with Rastermill calls, removes local Photon/parser fallback code and tests, and pins Rastermill to a GitHub tarball.

PR surface: Source -1044, Tests -662, Config -4, Other -7. Total -1717 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: current main has backend override, unavailable-optimizer passthrough, and best-effort EXIF normalization paths, while the PR source removes or bypasses them. I did not execute a live image flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review needs to decide the compatibility and supply-chain posture before merge; the remaining blockers are not a single safe autonomous repair loop.

target.title: Refactor image processing onto Rastermill
target.body:
## Summary

- Delegate OpenClaw image resize/encode/probe mechanics to Rastermill and remove local image parser/codec fallback code.
- Update OpenClaw to pushed Rastermill commit `89405b23b10e32bcbedbd0e2aa56ba790e94d148`, including `encodeBest`, `encodeToLimits`, `mimeType`, and Rastermill-owned transparency policy.
- Keep OpenClaw policy in OpenClaw: media caps, managed attachment warnings, SDK compatibility names, and orientation-aware managed attachment limits.

## Verification

- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Rastermill: `pnpm test && pnpm check && pnpm build && pnpm docs:build && git diff --check`
- Rastermill: `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after fixing accepted findings.
- OpenClaw: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/image-ops.rastermill-adapter.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism`
- OpenClaw: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism`
- OpenClaw: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- OpenClaw: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Image processing mechanics now live in Rastermill instead of OpenClaw-local parser/codec/fallback stacks.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/OpenClaw repo wrappers, Rastermill commit `89405b23b10e32bcbedbd0e2aa56ba790e94d148` pushed to `openclaw/rastermill`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest media/browser/agent/managed-image tests, full project typecheck, changed gate, Rastermill test/check/build/docs, and Codex autoreview on both repos.
Evidence after fix: Rastermill passed 2 files / 47 tests; OpenClaw focused media/browser/agent/managed-image set passed 7 files / 152 tests; managed/web subset passed 4 files / 143 tests; `check:changed` completed successfully including shrinkwrap guard, typecheck, lint, and import cycles.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw no longer owns image resize math or transparency-preservation preflight, consumes Rastermill `mimeType`, and delegates dimension-limited managed image encoding to `encodeToLimits` while keeping EXIF/display dimensions aligned for managed attachment limits.
What was not tested: no live HEIC conversion fixture against installed native tools in this PR run.
target.author: steipete
